Census & Demographic Data
=========================

Use this guide to identify appropriate sources for population and census data.

*   [Find US Data](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/us)
    *   [Census & ACS Guides & FAQs](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/faqs)

*   [Find DC Metro Area (DMV) & Virginia Data](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/c.php?g=120861&p=10786610)
*   [Find Datasets & Microdata](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/microdata "Datasets and microdata are set up to be used with statistical analysis software (SPSS, Stata, etc.).")
    *   [Datasets & Microdata](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/microdata#s-lg-box-2348375)

*   [Find International Data](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/international)
*   [Find Economic Data](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/economic-census)
*   [Related Guides](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/begin)

Datasets & Microdata
--------------------

The census data sources listed below are set up to be used with statistical analysis software (SPSS, Stata, etc.).

**United States**

*   **[Microdata Access Tool](https://data.census.gov/mdat/#/)**(MDAT)

 MDAT is the Census Bureau's microdata extraction tool. It allows you to select individual variables to develop customized tables and perform statistical analysis. Datasets included are: American Community Survey and the Current Population Survey.
*   **[IPUMS-USA](http://usa.ipums.org/usa/ "ipums")**(Minnesota Population Center)
*   Census microdata for social and economic research. Includes American Community Survey and Census of Population and Housing.
*   **[IPUMS-CPS](https://cps.ipums.org/cps/)**(Minnesota Population Center)

 Current Population Survey microdata. Can select variables and customize dataset before downloading.
*   **[U.S. Census Bureau Data Tools and Apps](http://www.census.gov/data/data-tools.html)**
*   **[American Community Survey Public Use Microdata Sample](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/microdata.html)** (PUMS)

 Direct download of PUMS data files from the Census Bureau's ftp site. Download files by Region, Division, State, or Public Use Microdata Areas (PUMAs). If you prefer to select variables for download, use IPUMS-USA or MDAT.
*   **[Census Bureau data via ftp](https://www2.census.gov/)**

 Direct download of data files from the Census Bureau's various survey programs.
*   ​**[Current Population Survey](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/cps.html)**(CPS)

 The primary source of labor force statistics for the population of the United States.
*   **[United States Census Data](https://census.icpsr.umich.edu/census/ "Census ICPSR")** Data Repository at ICPSR.

 Contains hundreds of data files from the U.S. Census Bureau. 
    *   **[Historical Demographic, Economic and Social Data](http://dx.doi.org/10.3886/ICPSR00003)**: the United States, 1790-1970.

 Collection of historical census data from the Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research (ICPSR).

*   **[Data Sharing for Demographic Research](https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/web/pages/DSDR/index.html)** (DSDR) A population health data archive at ICPSR.

**International**

*   **[IPUMS International](https://international.ipums.org/international/ "IPUMS International")**:68 countries -211 censuses -480 million person records
*   **[North Atlantic Population Project](https://www.nappdata.org/napp/ "NAPP")**: Census microdata from Canada, Great Britain, Germany, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, and the United States from 1801 to 1910.

*   [<<**Previous:** Find DC Metro Area (DMV) & Virginia Data](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/c.php?g=120861&p=10786610)
*   [**Next:** Find International Data >>](https://infoguides.gmu.edu/census-data/international)

--- SOURCE 3: Current Census Data Resources - U.S. Census Connections ---
URL: https://guides.loc.gov/census-connections/current-census-data

SUMMARY:
Current Census Data Resources - U.S. Census Connections: A Resource Guide - Research Guides at Library of Congress *   U.S. Census Bureau Data Sources *   Census Programs and Surveys (U.S. Census Bureau) The Census Bureau conducts more than 130 surveys and programs each year providing current demographic and economic data in-between censuses. *   My Congressional District (U.S. Census Bureau) Provides quick and easy access to demographic, social, economic, and housing statistics as well as data for businesses with paid employees at a detailed geography and industry level. *   QuickFacts (U.S. Census Bureau) This interactive site provides statistical data at the state, county and city levels with a population of 5,000 or more, providing information on demographics, the economy, housing, and businesses.

Current Census Data Resources
=============================

This page offers a selected list of census resources that provide current data from all the programs and surveys the United States Census Bureau administers. We recommend exploring the [U.S. Census Bureau website](https://www.census.gov/) for other tools and resources offered online. The U.S. Census Bureau also offers tutorials and webinars for the public to understand and find the data they seek.

*   [American Community Survey (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs) The American Community Survey (ACS) is a very important Census program that replaced the long form of the decennial census starting with the 2010 Census. It is an ongoing survey that collects detailed socioeconomic information about our communities. The ACS web page provides more information about the survey, data tables and guidance to locating and using data, and more.   
*   [Census Academy (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://www.census.gov/data/academy.html) This virtual hub offers training for learning data skills in all topical areas.   
*   [Census Programs and Surveys (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/surveys-programs.html) The Census Bureau conducts more than 130 surveys and programs each year providing current demographic and economic data in-between censuses. This page lists all of these programs and surveys.   
*   [Census Publications Library (U.S. Census Bureau)](http://census.gov/library/publications.html) This online library provides links to a variety of U.S. Bureau of the Census publications covering agriculture, government, business, trade and services, manufacturing & mineral industries, population and housing construction, foreign trade, transportation, as well as general and reference products and Technical Documentation/Working Papers.   
*   [Data.census.gov (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://data.census.gov/cedsci/) Data.census.gov is the new platform to access data and digital content from the U.S. Census Bureau. It replaced American FactFinder which was decommissioned in March 2020.   
*   [My Congressional District (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://www.census.gov/mycd/) Provides quick and easy access to demographic, social, economic, and housing statistics as well as data for businesses with paid employees at a detailed geography and industry level. The statistics are collected by the U.S. Census Bureau through the American Community Survey (ACS) and County Business Patterns (CBP).   
*   [QuickFacts (U.S. Census Bureau)](https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/fact/table/US/PST045218) This interactive site provides statistical data at the state, county and city levels with a population of 5,000 or more, providing information on demographics, the economy, housing, and businesses.   
*   [ResearchDataGov External](https://www.researchdatagov.org/) The site is the single portal for discovery of restricted data in the federal statistical system. The agencies, including the Census Bureau, have provided detailed descriptions of each data asset. Users can search for data by topic, agency, and keywords. In late 2022, users will be able to apply for access to these data using a single-application process built into ResearchDataGov. The database is built by and hosted at ICPSR at the University of Michigan, under contract and guidance from the National Center for Science and Engineering Statistics within the National Science Foundation.--_from website description_  

*   [<<**Previous:** History of the U.S. Census](https://guides.loc.gov/census-connections/census-history)
*   [**Next:** Census Reference Resources >>](https://guides.loc.gov/census-connections/census-reference)

--- SOURCE 5: IPUMS: Homepage ---
URL: https://www.ipums.org/

SUMMARY:
IPUMS provides census and survey data from around the world integrated across time and space. IPUMS integration and documentation makes it easy to study change.

FULL CONTENT:
![](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/styles/folwell_half/public/site-logo/logo-IPUMS%20%281%29_0.png?itok=JNUDv9k4)

IPUMS provides census and survey data from around the world integrated across time and space. IPUMS integration and documentation makes it easy to study change, conduct comparative research, merge information across data types, and analyze individuals within family and community contexts. Data and services available free of charge.

![IPUMS USA](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-usa-dark-342x96_0.png)

U.S. Census and American Community Survey microdata from 1850 to the present. [Learn More about the IPUMS USA project](/projects/ipums-usa "Learn More about the IPUMS USA Project")

![IPUMS CPS](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-cps-dark-337x96.png)

Current Population Survey microdata including basic monthly surveys and supplements from 1962 to the present. [Learn More about the IPUMS CPS project](/projects/ipums-cps "Learn More about the IPUMS CPS Project")

![IPUMS International](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-international-dark-461x96.png)

World's largest collection of census microdata covering over 100 countries, contemporary and historical. [Learn More about the IPUMS International Project](/projects/ipums-international "Learn More about the IPUMS International Project")

![IPUMS Global Health](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-global-health-dark-436x96.png)

Health survey data from around the world, including harmonized data collections for [Visit the IPUMS DHS site](https://www.idhsdata.org/ "Visit the IPUMS DHS Site"), [MICS](https://mics.ipums.org/mics/ "Visit the IPUMS MICS Site"), and [Visit the PMA site](https://pma.ipums.org/ "Visit the IPUMS PMA Site"). [Learn More about the IPUMS Global Health Projects](/projects/ipums-global-health "Learn More about the IPUMS Global Health Projects")

![IPUMS NHGIS](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-nhgis-dark-337x96.png)

U.S. Census summary tables and GIS data from 1790 to the present. [Learn More about the IPUMS NHGIS Project](/projects/ipums-nhgis "Learn More about the IPUMS NHGIS Project")

![IPUMS IHGIS](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/ipums-ihgis_n_rgb_337px.png)

Summary tables and GIS data from population, housing, and agricultural censuses around the world. [Learn More about the IPUMS IHGIS Project](/projects/ipums-ihgis "Learn More about the IPUMS IHGIS Project")

![IPUMS Time Use](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-time-use-dark-246x70.png)

Historical and contemporary time use data from 1930 to the present. [Learn More about the IPUMS Time Use Projects](/projects/ipums-time-use "Learn More about the IPUMS Time Use Projects")

![IPUMS Health Surveys](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-health-surveys-dark-484x96.png)

Historical and contemporary U.S. health survey data from [Visit the IPUMS NHIS site](https://nhis.ipums.org/nhis/ "Visit the IPUMS NHIS Site") (1963-present) and [Visit the IPUMS MEPS site](https://meps.ipums.org/meps/ "Visit the IPUMS MEPS Site") (1996-present). [Learn More about the IPUMS Health Surveys Projects](/projects/ipums-health-surveys "Learn More about the IPUMS Health Surveys Projects")

![IPUMS Higher Ed](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/project-logo/logo-higher-ed-dark-350x96.png)

Survey data on the science and engineering workforce in the U.S. from 1993 to the present. [Learn More about the IPUMS Higher Ed Project](/projects/ipums-higher-ed "Learn More about the IPUMS Higher Ed Project")

![](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/styles/folwell_third/public/2021-06/logo-blog.png?itok=12RsI47s)

## [IPUMS MLP: Revolutionizing Linked Data](https://blog.popdata.org/ipums-mlp-linked-data/ )

As researchers, we often ask questions we cannot answer due to lack of data. More intriguingly, however, there are questions we only think of asking once we encounter data that may answer them. Good data address existing problems; great data inspire new questions. The latest iteration of the IPUMS Multigenerational Longitudinal Panel (MLP) project, which links together records from the full count US census data, fits this description. Visit our data browser and project description for inspiration on research questions you did not know could be asked – and answered.

Full count census data offer unprecedented opportunities for social scientific research. Once harmonized, these data enable precise measurement of key demographic, economic, and social patterns across time and space. Researchers can observe entire populations over long periods and produce estimates virtually free of sampling error. Estimates can also be produced down to the smallest geographical units, allowing researchers to define and observe communities with an outstanding level of detail.

([Read More](https://blog.popdata.org/ipums-mlp-linked-data/ "Read more about IPUMS MLP: Revolutionizing Linked Data"))

![Core Trust Seal logo with a circle around the words and a golden checkmark.](/sites/www.ipums.org/files/styles/folwell_circle/public/2024-05/coretrustseal-logo-transparent.png?itok=pEgBL4Lz)

IPUMS is certified by Core Trust Seal based on the Core Trustworthy Data Repositories Requirements. Learn more about the certification at [coretrustseal.org](https://coretrustseal.org).

enriched_points.expanded_content: Surveys, such as the American Community Survey, provide detailed demographic insights by collecting data directly from individuals, enabling analysis of socioeconomic characteristics across various populations.

Census results, including the decennial U.S. Census, offer comprehensive demographic data on population size, distribution, and characteristics, serving as a foundational resource for demographic research.
Public records, encompassing vital statistics like birth and death registrations, provide historical and current demographic information that supports research on population trends and patterns.
